metadata.page_title: Imperial Fists Space Marine Army List

special_rules.name: Special Rules
special_rules.text: The They Shall Know No Fear rule applies to all Imperial Fist detachment, aircraft and static defence formations (see They Shall Know No Fear ). The Fortified Positions rule applies to Imperial Fist Space Marine armies (see Fortified Positions ).

special_rules.name: They Shall Know No Fear
special_rules.text:
How many Blast markers does a broken Space Marine unit count as having when working out the result of an assault?
One per unit.
Space Marines are renowned for their tenacity and bravery. This is represented by the following rules:
* It takes 2 Blast markers to suppress a Space Marine unit or kill a unit in a broken formation (ignore any leftover Blast markers).
* Space Marine formations are only broken if they have 2 Blast markers per unit in the formation.
* Space Marines formations only count half their number of Blaster markers in assault resolution (rounding down—note that assault resolution will not receive +1 for having no Blast markers if the formation has 1 Blast marker before rounding down). Halve the number of extra hits suffered by a Space Marine formation that loses an assault, rounding down in favour of the Space Marines.
* When a broken Space Marine formation rallies, it receives a number of Blast markers equal to the number of units, rather than half this number. Space Marines with the Leader special ability remove 2 Blast markers instead of 1.

profiles.name: Techmarine
abilities_or_notes: If added to a Bastion unit the Bastion gains 1 Void Shield.

abilities_or_notes: Counts as a Terminator unit for the purposes of being transported.

abilities_or_notes: Counts as a Terminator unit for the purposes of being transported.

profiles.name: Tarantula Platform
abilities_or_notes: Armed with either a twin heavy bolter or a twin lascannon. Units armed with a Twin Heavy Bolter have their firefight value increased to 5+.
ambiguity_reasons: Armed with either a twin heavy bolter or a twin lascannon. Units armed with a Twin Heavy Bolter have their firefight value increased to 5+.

profiles.name: Hellfire Dreadnought
abilities_or_notes: Counts as a Dreadnought unit for the purposes of being transported.

profiles.name: Land Raider
abilities_or_notes: May transport one Terminator unit or two infantry units without Jump Packs or Mounted .

profiles.name: Land Raider Achilles
abilities_or_notes: May transport one Terminator unit or two infantry units without Jump Packs or Mounted .
parse_warnings: Boolean weapon line folded into previous weapon for Land Raider Achilles.
Boolean weapon line folded into previous weapon for Land Raider Achilles.

profiles.name: Land Raider Crusader
abilities_or_notes: May transport three infantry units without Jump Packs or Mounted ; Terminator units count as one and a half units each, rounding up. Counts as a Land Raider for the purposes of being transported.

profiles.name: Razorback
abilities_or_notes: May transport one infantry unit (except a Terminator unit) without Jump Packs or Mounted . Armed with either a Twin Lascannon or a Twin Heavy Bolter. Razorbacks armed with a Twin Heavy Bolter have their firefight value increased to 4+.
ambiguity_reasons: May transport one infantry unit (except a Terminator unit) without Jump Packs or Mounted . Armed with either a Twin Lascannon or a Twin Heavy Bolter. Razorbacks armed with a Twin Heavy Bolter have their firefight value increased to 4+.

profiles.name: Rhino
abilities_or_notes: May transport two infantry units (except Terminator units) without Jump Packs or Mounted .

profiles.name: Siege Dreadnought
abilities_or_notes: Counts as a Dreadnought unit for the purposes of being transported.
parse_warnings: Boolean weapon line folded into previous weapon for Siege Dreadnought.
Boolean weapon line folded into previous weapon for Siege Dreadnought.

profiles.name: Bastion
abilities_or_notes: May transport eight infantry units or Dreadnoughts; Terminator units and Dreadnoughts count as two units each. Transported units do not have to disembark in order to shoot or use their firefight value if this unit is able to shoot, assault or lend support. The unit counts as rubble once it is destroyed. Critical Hit Effect: The unit takes an extra point of damage.
parse_warnings: Boolean weapon line folded into previous weapon for Bastion.

profiles.name: Fellblade
abilities_or_notes: Critical Hit Effect: Destroyed. All units within 5cm of the model suffer a hit on a roll of 6+.

profiles.name: Reaver Class Titan
abilities_or_notes: May step over units and pieces of terrain that that are lower lower than the unit's knees and less than 2cm wide. Critical Hit Effect: The unit's plasma reactor has been breached. Roll a D6 for each breach on the unit in the end phase of each turn. On a roll of 1 the unit is destroyed and all units within 5cm suffer a hit on a roll of 5+, and on a roll of 4-6 the breach has been repaired. Any breach not repaired causes one point of damage.

profiles.name: Warlord Class Titan
abilities_or_notes: May step over units and pieces of terrain that that are lower lower than the unit's knees and less than 2cm wide. Critical Hit Effect: The unit's plasma reactor has been breached. Roll a D6 for each breach on the unit in the end phase of each turn. On a roll of 1 the unit is destroyed and all units within 5cm suffer a hit on a roll of 4+, and on a roll of 4-6 the breach has been repaired. Any breach not repaired causes one point of damage.

army_notes.name: Using The Army List
army_notes.text:
The following army list allows you to field an army based on a Imperial Fists Space Marine army. It can also be used as a stand in army list for any of the Imperial Fists’ successor chapters.
Space Marines are organised into small formations called detachments . Each detachment is made up of one or more units, and may also include a number of extra units called upgrades .
The detachments that may be taken in a Imperial Fists army are shown on the chart that follows. The chart also shows what units comprise the detachment, what upgrades are allowed, and its points cost.
Each upgrade that is taken adds to the cost of the detachment, as shown on the upgrade chart . The upgrade chart also lists the units that make up the upgrade. Note that these will sometimes replace units in the detachment they are taken for, and sometimes takes the form of additional units for the detachment. Each upgrade may be taken once per formation.
Imperial Fist armies may be supported by Titan Legion battlegroups, and Imperial Fist aircraft and static defence formations. A maximum of up to a third of the points available to the army may be spent on these formations.
